title: Ch 9.3 — Custom Exceptions: Evil Loan Approval
description:
Practice creating a custom exception class and using throws / throw.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Background
──────────────────────────────────────────────

The Bank of Evil needs a loan approval system. When an application
is invalid, we want to throw our own exception type — LoanException
— with a clear message explaining why.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Step 1 — Create LoanException
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Write a class called LoanException that extends Exception.
  Give it one constructor:

    public LoanException(String message) {
        super(message);
    }

  That's it — three lines total (class + constructor + super call).
  Because it extends Exception (not RuntimeException), the compiler
  will require callers to catch or declare it.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
Step 2 — Complete LoanApprovalSystem
──────────────────────────────────────────────

  Method 1:
    String validateLoan(String villainName, int amount, int score)
    throws LoanException

    • If villainName is null or blank, throw:
        new LoanException("Villain name required")
    • If amount < 1000, throw:
        new LoanException("Amount too small")
    • If score < 1 || score > 10, throw:
        new LoanException("Score must be 1-10")
    • If all valid, return:
        villainName + " approved for $" + amount

  Method 2:
    String processApplication(String name, int amount, int score)

    Use try-catch-finally:
    • TRY: call validateLoan() and set result to its return value.
    • CATCH LoanException: set result = "Denied: " + e.getMessage()
    • FINALLY: result += " (done)"
    • Return result.

──────────────────────────────────────────────
HINTS
──────────────────────────────────────────────
• LoanException must appear BEFORE LoanApprovalSystem in the file
  (Java reads top to bottom).
• Use String.isBlank() to check for empty/whitespace names.
• Declare  String result = "";  before the try block.
• validateLoan throws — processApplication catches.
